reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (headless Chrome, real inference via local HF mirror + CDP request-rewrite, light+dark, mobile+desktop, full cache-state matrix) — revision 1

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — REAL transformers.js@3.7.5 run in a Web Worker (models/nougat-ocr/worker.js → lib/webai.js loadPipeline, task image-to-text, Xenova/nougat-small, dtype q8, WASM). End-to-end on the ACTUAL page (fresh profile → Download ~250 MB, no auto-download → auto-init ready → Transcribe): the paper sample returned real Markdown streamed over 187 tokens in 13048 ms WASM (14.3 tok/s), rendered output began '# On-Device Neural Transcription of Scientific Documents … ###### Abstract … We study whether a compact vision-encoder-decoder …'. Pre-verified independently the same pipeline on the real Nougat paper image → '# Nougat: Neural Optical Understanding for Academic Documents / Lukas Blecher / … / ###### Abstract'. screenshots: /home/paulkinlan/tmp/nougat-evidence/{01-download-state,02-ready,03-output-light,04-output-dark,06-cached-autoinit,light-wild}.png
rubric.notes: DISTINCT from TrOCR (single printed LINE) and Donut (document-VQA answering a question OCR-free): Nougat transcribes a WHOLE research PAGE into structured Markdown with LaTeX math preserved. VisionEncoderDecoder (donut-swin encoder 896×672 + mBART decoder), same family as Donut — that is why it is transformers.js-runnable where DePlot/Pix2Struct is not.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — output is [{generated_text: markdown}]; the worker returns the trimmed markdown and the page renders it (headings/paragraphs/lists/LaTeX-tables/math) plus a raw view. Semantic sanity holds across all three samples: paper → title #, ###### Abstract, ## 1 Introduction; equations page → 3 display \[…\] + 2 inline \(…\) LaTeX spans (readout 'display equations 3 · inline math 2', renderer produced 3 .math-display + 2 .math-inline nodes; recovered LaTeX e.g. \text{Attention}(Q,K,V)=\text{softmax}(\frac{QK^{\top}}{\sqrt{d_k}})V and \theta^{*}=\arg\min_{\theta}[\frac{1}{N}\sum…]); table page → \begin{tabular} parsed to a 5-column, 4-row HTML grid + CSV. conformance io-shape-semantic=manual (agent verdict recorded).
rubric.notes: Nougat renders TABLES as LaTeX \begin{tabular} (not Markdown pipes) — the practical page + nougat.js parseLatexTable/latexTableToCsv handle exactly that real output shape.

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): declares-model-id=pass (Xenova/nougat-small linked to card + facebook/nougat-small), declares-task=pass (image-to-text), declares-backend=pass (WebAssembly), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker via NougatEngine). At-a-glance also states the architecture (donut-swin 896×672 → mBART, ~250M params), the ~250 MB (77 MB encoder + 165 MB decoder) download split, and CC-BY-4.0.
rubric.notes: Runnable on the shared 3.7.5 pin — no version-pin needed. NougatImageProcessor + NougatTokenizer + VisionEncoderDecoderModel + the image-to-text pipeline all exist in 3.7.5 (grep-confirmed in dist bundle); the processor resolved cleanly (no 'Unknown image_processor_type'), unlike Pix2Struct/DePlot.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — full state matrix on the actual page (model served from a local HF mirror via CDP request-rewrite so cache keys stay huggingface.co/Xenova/nougat-small/…): first visit (fresh profile) shows state=download-required with a single 'Download model (~250 MB)' action and does NOT auto-download; clicking streams progress and the shared loader auto-inits to 'Model ready'. Reload in the same profile → state=ready with NO Download button (only 'Clear cached model'), run enabled — the cached current version auto-initialises. Cache readout: 'Xenova/nougat-small · transformers.js · ~250 MB · 316 MB cached of 11054 MB'. conformance shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual.
rubric.notes: Uses the shared createModelLoader/model-cache architecture unchanged (needle /Xenova/nougat-small/ matches the cached Cache-Storage keys). All 5 pages (index + basics/practical/wild/multi-model) mount createModelLoader; multi-model mounts TWO loaders (Nougat + DistilBART) and only enables Run when both are ready.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — download progress streamed via the shared loader; Retry surfaced by the loader on failure; transcription errors go to a .status.err region ('Transcription failed: …'); runs on the WASM path with no GPU (headless no-GPU produced real output). Load/run status announced in role=status aria-live=polite regions. conformance live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.
rubric.notes: requiresWebGPU:false — WASM everywhere; no fake fallback.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — every visible control operable: keyboard-operable dropzone + sample thumbnails (Enter/Space), Transcribe button, max-output-length slider (live label, drove 64–600 tokens), Rendered/Raw segmented toggle (aria-pressed), Copy Markdown/CSV/LaTeX chips (all ≥36px). Practical page adds table→Markdown + table→CSV copy; wild page adds copy-all-LaTeX; multi-model chains the two Run stages. conformance controls-operable=manual.
rubric.notes: Verified Rendered/Raw toggle flips output (raw view showed '# On-Device… ###### Abstract'); wild 'Copy all LaTeX' populated with the recovered equations.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — every character shown comes from the on-device worker run (verified real markdown streamed token-by-token; 187-token trace on the paper, 3+2 math spans on the equations page). No canned output anywhere; the multi-model summary is a real DistilBART run over the real Nougat text. conformance no-fake-output=manual.

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. role=status aria-live loader + run status; sample thumbnails and dropzone are keyboard-operable (Enter/Space) with visible focus; the Rendered/Raw toggle is a labelled role=group with aria-pressed; chips/buttons meet ≥36px min-block-size.
rubric.notes: responsive-check reported desktop+mobile clean; no sub-24px actionable targets flagged.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — desktop 1280×900 (dark) and light 1280 render with no horizontal overflow; mobile 360×740 DPR3 measured 0px horizontal overflow. The two-column .field-row uses flex-wrap with flex:1 1 320px + min-inline-size:0 so the page image and output stack on narrow screens; the LaTeX-math boxes and tables scroll inside overflow-x:auto wrappers (long unbreakable LaTeX never spills the page). responsive-check programmatic pass on both classes; screenshots reviewed. conformance responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual.

Agent verdict — all inference (donut-swin encode + mBART autoregressive decode) runs in a Web Worker (NougatEngine); the control UI stayed responsive during the 13–16 s WASM runs (CDP main-thread evals kept returning). Tokens stream to the trace incrementally rather than in one blocking write; the markdown render is a single cheap string pass on completion. 0 console errors across index + wild. conformance inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: Nougat is a page-scale generator so a full transcription takes 10–16 s WASM — but it is entirely off-thread, so INP is unaffected; the at-a-glance + loader state the ~250 MB / multi-second cost honestly.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two-column layout without horizontal overflow for an image-to-markdown OCR demo
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing + flexible tracks (flex/minmax) before fixed widths; use flex-wrap on control/columns rows; set min-inline-size:0 and overflow-wrap on items with long unbreakable content; scroll wide content inside its own overflow-x:auto container.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the run panel is a .field-row flex-wrap with two .field-col (flex:1 1 320px, min-inline-size:0) so the page image and output stack on mobile; the rendered LaTeX-math boxes (.math-display) and tables (.md-table-wrap) use overflow-x:auto so long LaTeX/wide tables scroll inside instead of overflowing the page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; e2e measured 0px horizontal overflow at 360×740 DPR3

guidanceConsulted.query: run inference in a web worker keep main thread responsive stream progress
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy/background work off the main thread so the UI stays responsive; post incremental progress.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all Nougat inference runs in a dedicated module Web Worker (NougatEngine ⇄ worker.js); tokens are streamed back via a TextStreamer and the multi-model page runs Nougat then DistilBART, both off-thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0 console errors; main-thread CDP evals stayed responsive during the 13–16 s WASM runs; 187-token stream rendered incrementally

guidanceConsulted.query: stream progress without INP regressions during a long page transcription
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread work / keep it off-thread and yield so input + rendering stay responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the page-scale generation loop is entirely in the worker; the main thread only appends one token span per streamed token and renders the markdown once at the end (a single cheap pass), so no long main-thread task blocks INP.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: UI responsive during the run; token trace updated live without freezing the controls

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live status region loading progress and keyboard-operable controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Label every control; announce state in a role=status aria-live region; keyboard operability + visible focus for custom controls; adequate tap targets.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + run status are role=status aria-live=polite; dropzone and sample thumbnails handle Enter/Space with visible focus; the Rendered/Raw toggle is a labelled role=group with aria-pressed; chips/buttons meet ≥36px.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page nav.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — @view-transition navigation:auto in the shared public/styles.css; every Nougat page links it and uses the shared design system (no raw hex for text/bg).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; light+dark both render with design-system tokens (WCAG-AA)

openQuestions: Nougat occasionally makes small symbol slips on synthetic MathML-rendered pages (Q→\mathcal{Q}, ℓ→\Phi, x→\chi). Would a confidence/uncertainty surface (e.g. per-token generation probability from the streamer) help the reader spot where to double-check the LaTeX?

Should the demo add real scanned open-access arXiv pages (CC-BY) alongside the self-authored synthetic samples, to show performance on true PDF renders — while keeping the rights-safe bundled set as the default?
Nougat-base (~350M, ~350 MB) recovers more math than nougat-small — worth a size/quality toggle, or does the small model keep the honest browser download cost low enough?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a Nougat→translation multi-model rung (transcribe a foreign-language page, then translate the prose with M2M100) to complement the transcribe→summarise composition.
Surface per-token generation confidence in the See-inside trace so readers can spot low-confidence math spans to verify.
Add a rights-safe gallery of real open-access (CC-BY) scanned arXiv pages as optional samples, keeping the synthetic set as the default.
